uplink.description: 0x6d periodic data, two samples (newest-first); newest -> air.* PM, tvoc -> tvocMassConcentration, older -> samples
uplink._note: TTN catalog example input for vendor/adeunis/breath. Wire values confirmed by running reference/upstream-codec.js (tvoc 0x01B3=435, pm10 0x003E=62, pm25 0x001E=30, pm1 0x0014=20; status 0x80 -> frameCounter 4, all flags clear). Upstream nests these under data.bytes with per-channel arrays; we map the newest sample to air.* and keep TVOC as the µg/m³ extra tvocMassConcentration (air.tvoc is ppb).

uplink.description: 0x6d single sample with status flags set (frameCounter 1, lowBattery, configurationDone, sensorError)
uplink._note: Synthetic single-sample frame; expected produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js. Status byte 0x23 -> frameCounter (0x23&0xe0)>>5=1, lowBattery (0x23&0x02), configurationDone (0x23&0x01), configurationInconsistency (0x23&0x08)=0, sensorError (0x23&0x16)=0x02 -> true. Upstream uses the 0x16 sensorError mask verbatim; we mirror it.

uplink.description: 0x6e alarm frame, all channels active -> air.* PM + alarms extra
uplink._note: Synthetic alarm frame; wire values confirmed by running reference/upstream-codec.js (flags byte 0x0f -> all four channels active; tvoc 0x01B3=435 at offset 3, pm10 0x003E=62 at 5, pm25 0x001E=30 at 7, pm1 0x0014=20 at 9; status 0x00 all clear).

uplink.description: 0x30 daily frame (long form) -> per-channel min/max/average aggregates in daily extra; no instantaneous air.*
uplink._note: Synthetic long-form daily frame; aggregate values confirmed by running reference/upstream-codec.js (tvoc min/max/avg/dur = 1/5/10/3 at offsets 2,4,6,8; pm10 = 2/8/4/3 at 10..16; pm25 = 1/4/2/3 at 18..24; pm1 = 1/3/2 at 26,28,30, no duration). Daily aggregates carry no instantaneous reading, so nothing maps to air.*.

uplink.description: Unsupported frame code (0x20 configuration) returns an error
uplink._note: Synthetic; the 0x20 configuration frame carries no measurement and is not normalized (upstream returns it under data.bytes, but our normalized codec only emits the 0x6d/0x6e/0x30 measurement frames).
